2603.23483.title: SpecEyes: Accelerating Agentic Multimodal LLMs via Speculative Perception and Planning
2603.23483.paper_summary: Agentic multimodal large language models (MLLMs) (e.g., OpenAI o3 and Gemini Agentic Vision) achieve remarkable reasoning capabilities through iterative visual tool invocation. However, the cascaded perception, reasoning, and tool-calling loops introduce significant sequential overhead. This overhead, termed agentic depth, incurs prohibitive latency and seriously limits system-level concurrency. To this end, we propose SpecEyes, an agentic-level speculative acceleration framework that breaks this sequential bottleneck. Our key insight is that a lightweight, tool-free MLLM can serve as a speculative planner to predict the execution trajectory, enabling early termination of expensive tool chains without sacrificing accuracy. To regulate this speculative planning, we introduce a cognitive gating mechanism based on answer separability, which quantifies the model's confidence for self-verification without requiring oracle labels. Furthermore, we design a heterogeneous parallel funnel that exploits the stateless concurrency of the small model to mask the stateful serial execution of the large model, maximizing system throughput. Extensive experiments on V* Bench, HR-Bench, and POPE demonstrate that SpecEyes achieves 1.1-3.35x speedup over the agentic baseline while preserving or even improving accuracy (up to +6.7%), thereby boosting serving throughput under concurrent workloads.
2603.23483.paper_summary_zh: 代理多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）（例如，OpenAI o3 和 Gemini Agentic Vision）通過迭代的視覺工具調用實現了卓越的推理能力。<lb/>然而，級聯的感知、推理和工具調用迴圈引入了顯著的序列開銷。<lb/>這種開銷被稱為代理深度，會產生過高的延遲，並嚴重限制系統級的並發性。<lb/>為此，我們提出了 SpecEyes，一個代理級的推測加速框架，旨在打破這一序列瓶頸。<lb/>我們的關鍵見解是，輕量級的無工具 MLLM 可以作為推測規劃者來預測執行軌跡，從而在不犧牲準確性的情況下，提前終止昂貴的工具鏈。<lb/>為了調節這一推測規劃，我們引入了一種基於答案可分離性的認知閘機制，該機制量化模型的自我驗證信心，而不需要神諭標籤。<lb/>此外，我們設計了一個異構並行漏斗，利用小模型的無狀態並發性來掩蓋大模型的有狀態串行執行，從而最大化系統吞吐量。<lb/>在 V* Bench、HR-Bench 和 POPE 上的廣泛實驗表明，SpecEyes 在保持或甚至提高準確性（最高可達 +6.7%）的同時，實現了相對於代理基線的 1.1-3.35 倍加速，從而在並發工作負載下提升了服務吞吐量。
2603.23483.author: Haoyu Huang et.al.
2603.23483.authors: Haoyu Huang, Jinfa Huang, Zhongwei Wan, Xiawu Zheng, Rongrong Ji, Jiebo Luo
